loopStyle: Synchronous turn loop (ConversationRuntime::run_turn — loop{} over api_client.stream, capped by max_iterations); each model-requested tool runs PreToolUse hook → mode-based permission policy (read-only / workspace-write / danger-full-access / prompt / allow) → execute → PostToolUse hook

nodes.label: run_turn(user_input)
nodes.note: **ConversationRuntime::run_turn** records the turn and pushes the user text onto the session (conversation.rs:337). If the session was compacted, a **session-health probe** runs first (conversation.rs:326). The `claw` REPL (rusty-claude-cli) drives this with an interactive PermissionPrompter.

nodes.label: Turn loop (loop {})
nodes.note: The turn loop. `iterations += 1`; if it exceeds **max_iterations** the turn fails (conversation.rs:349). Each pass builds an ApiRequest from the system prompt + full message history and calls the model. Auto-compaction is checked every iteration (conversation.rs:403).

nodes.label: api_client.stream
nodes.note: **api_client.stream(request)** runs one streaming generation via the `api` crate (Anthropic / OpenAI-compatible / xAI). The AssistantEvent stream is folded by **build_assistant_message** (conversation.rs:368) into content blocks + usage + prompt-cache telemetry.

nodes.label: Extract ToolUse blocks
nodes.note: The assistant message is pushed to the session; **pending_tool_uses** are filtered out of its `ToolUse` blocks (conversation.rs:380). If there are **none**, the loop breaks and the turn is complete (conversation.rs:407); otherwise each tool-use is processed in order.

nodes.label: PreToolUse hook
nodes.note: Before permission, **run_pre_tool_use_hook** fires (conversation.rs:412). A hook can **rewrite the input**, supply a **permission override** (Allow / Deny / Ask), or **cancel/deny** the call outright (conversation.rs:421-441) — in which case it short-circuits to a denied tool result without consulting the policy.

nodes.label: PermissionPolicy.authorize
nodes.note: **authorize_with_context** resolves the outcome under a **PermissionMode** — `read-only`, `workspace-write`, `danger-full-access`, `prompt`, or `allow` (permissions.rs:9) — plus per-tool requirements and config rules (permissions.rs:181). In **prompt** mode it consults the interactive **PermissionPrompter** (permissions.rs:281), whose Allow/Deny decision becomes the outcome; **allow**/**danger-full-access** auto-allow; hook **Ask/Deny/Allow** overrides take precedence. Result is **Allow** or **Deny { reason }**.

nodes.label: tool_executor.execute (+PostToolUse)
nodes.note: On **Allow**, **tool_executor.execute(tool_name, input)** runs the tool (bash, file ops, glob/grep, MCP-bridged tools, etc.). A **PostToolUse** hook then runs (conversation.rs:475) — or a failure hook on error — and can mark the result an error and append feedback. The output becomes a tool_result.

nodes.label: Permission denied
nodes.note: A **Deny** (from the policy, a prompt rejection, or a PreToolUse hook) produces an **error tool_result** carrying the reason and any hook feedback (conversation.rs:498). The tool does not run; the error is fed back to the model and the loop continues.

nodes.label: Push tool_result
nodes.note: The result message (success or error) is pushed onto the session and recorded (conversation.rs:505). After all pending tool-uses are processed, control returns to the top of the loop, which re-prompts the model with the results in context.

nodes.label: Turn complete (TurnSummary)
nodes.note: When an assistant turn carried **no tool-use blocks**, the loop breaks and a **TurnSummary** (assistant messages, tool results, usage, prompt-cache events, auto-compaction) is returned (conversation.rs:513).

scenarios.title: Model answers with **no tools**
scenarios.note: The first assistant turn carries only text — no `ToolUse` blocks — so pending_tool_uses is empty, the loop breaks, and run_turn returns the TurnSummary.

scenarios.title: User asks to **edit a file**
scenarios.note: The model emits an edit/write tool-use. The PreToolUse hook passes; the permission policy authorizes it (auto in **allow** mode, or via the prompter in **prompt** mode). The tool runs, PostToolUse fires, the result is pushed, the loop re-prompts, and the model finishes.

scenarios.title: **Tool call is denied**
scenarios.note: The policy returns **Deny** — because the mode/rules forbid the tool, the interactive prompter rejected it, or (equivalently) a PreToolUse hook denied it. An error tool_result with the reason is fed back; the model adapts on the next turn and finishes.

scenarios.title: Model issues **several tool calls**
scenarios.note: One assistant turn carries multiple `ToolUse` blocks. claw-code processes them **sequentially** in a `for` loop (conversation.rs:411): each runs its own PreToolUse hook, permission check, execution, and PostToolUse hook before the next. All results are pushed, then the loop re-prompts and the model finishes.
